text.title: Stripe Webhook Hardening Failure Modes
text.summary: Antipatterns observed in real deployments of Stripe Webhook Hardening. Each ties a symptom to a root cause and a concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Tolerance set to 1 hour to mask clock drift; captured event replayed days later, processed again.
antipattern.root-cause: SDK default flipped manually to mask infra issue.
antipattern.fix: Restore tolerance to 5 minutes; fix clock drift at infra layer (NTP); revoke any suspect events.

antipattern.symptom: Secret in env var for 2 years; exposed via leaked log; forged events accepted.
antipattern.root-cause: No rotation runbook; rotation deemed risky.
antipattern.fix: Build rotation runbook with dual-secret window; rotate; document cadence; alert on age.

antipattern.symptom: FastAPI default body parser ran before verify; signature fails on canonicalised JSON.
antipattern.root-cause: Route used default request.json before construct_event.
antipattern.fix: Read raw body bytes; pass to construct_event; only json.loads after verify success.

antipattern.symptom: Verify loop saturates CPU under forge-attempt flood; legitimate events delayed.
antipattern.root-cause: No edge rate limit; compute is the only chokepoint.
antipattern.fix: Configure edge rate-limit at 200 rpm per IP; add 429 + retry-after; alert on sustained 4xx.
